provenance.source_corpus: Peatland restoration in southern Québec (Canada): A paleoecological perspective>

nodes.name: release (biomass burning) or input (fertilization) of nutrients

source_spans.text: In both cases, the release (biomass burning) or input (fertilization) of nutrients strongly stimulates the growth of Polytrichum strictum.
In both cases, the release (biomass burning) or input (fertilization) of nutrients strongly stimulates the growth of Polytrichum strictum.
The release of nutrients from the burning of plant tissues is probably the main reason for the sudden growth of Polytrichum strictum populations in fire-disturbed bogs.
In mined bogs, P. strictum helps stabilize the soil surface, prevents wind erosion and frost heaving, and seems to favour the subsequent establishment of Sphagnum colonies
In mined bogs, P. strictum helps stabilize the soil surface, prevents wind erosion and frost heaving, and seems to favour the subsequent establishment of Sphagnum colonies
Therefore, it is possible that the restoration method used in eastern Canada will result in rapid vegetation succession culminating in a Sphagnum-dominated peatland.
Therefore, it is possible that the restoration method used in eastern Canada will result in rapid vegetation succession culminating in a Sphagnum-dominated peatland.
